item.title: Ch'ŭgugi
item.description: Ch'ŭgugi (Korean: 측우기; Hanja: 測雨器; RR: cheugugi) were rain gauges invented and used during the Joseon dynasty of Korea. They were invented and supplied to each provincial office during the reign of King Sejong the Great. As of 2010, only one ch'ŭgugi remains, known as the Geumyeong Cheugugi (금영측우기; 錦營測雨器), which literally means "ch'ŭgugi installed on the provincial office's yard." It is designated as National Treasure #561 of Korea and was installed in the provincial office of Gongju city, 1837 by King Yeongjo.[citation needed] In addition, the official measure of rainfall by ch'ŭgugi from King Jeongjo's reign to Emperor Gojong's reign is preserved.

item.title: Mark Kadin
item.description: Mark L'vovich Kadin (Russian: Марк Львович Кадин; born April 28, 1965) is a Russian conductor and musical director of Soviet origin.[1][2][3]

item.title: EVOS Esports
item.description: EVOS Esports (briefly known as Zero Latitude) is an Indonesian esports organisation based in Jakarta. It currently has competitive teams in Apex Legends, Call of Duty: Warzone, Cross Fire, Free Fire, and Mobile Legends: Bang Bang.[1][2]

item.title: Zhou Boqi
item.description: Zhou Boqi Chinese: 周伯琦 (1298–1369) was a Chinese court poet during the Yuan Dynasty.[1] Zhou was born in Raozhou (now Boyang) but grew up in Beijing, and in his early career worked there as a magistrate and later as Senior Compiler for Imperial Academy.[2] He was well known for his seal script calligraphy.[2][3]

item.title: QF 4-inch naval gun Mk XIX
item.description: The QF 4-inch Mk XIX gun[note 1] was a British low-velocity 4-inch 40-calibre naval gun used to arm small warships such as Bathurst and Castle-class corvette and some River-class frigate in World War II, mainly against submarines.[3]
